what_it_does: Defines a static 1600x900 HTML/CSS composition for a warm immersive studio/atelier hero screen with branded wordmark, mission headline, info callout, hotspot dots, menu pill, monogram mark, blueprint texture, light-pool background, and a thin hatched footer band.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Builds the main visual field with layered radial gradients, repeating linear gradients, masks, opacity, and blend modes rather than image assets.
Positions all visible elements absolutely inside a fixed-size .view container.
Includes Studio V branding, an avatar glyph, a mission headline, an info card, three hotspot markers, a menu control, a V mark, and a footer hatch band.
Contains CSS comments documenting design intent, including warm light pool, blueprint texture, spatial markers, and a v2.1 footer correction.
The final .gband override changes the earlier 100px descriptive footer into a 26px semi-transparent gold hatch and hides its child content.
standouts: Warm light-pool background is expressed entirely through CSS gradients and color-mix variables.
The file contains an explicit late-stage correction that materially overrides earlier footer-band styling.
The composition combines interior-design language with interface-like markers and callouts.
conventions: Self-contained single HTML document with embedded CSS and static markup.
Uses design-token CSS variables for color, spacing, radius, and type scale.
Uses absolute positioning for layout within a fixed artboard-like viewport.
Uses comments as design rationale and revision notes.
Breaks semantic document structure in favor of presentation-focused art direction.
concerns: Fixed 1600x900 sizing does not include responsive behavior in this file.
Several visible elements depend on external design variables, so rendering depends on /surface/styles/vars.css being present.
The .gband rules are duplicated, with the later v2.1 block overriding the earlier implementation via !important.
The CSS comment says invented copy is hidden, but the hidden footer text remains in the DOM.
notes: A static warm studio hero mockup where CSS-generated atmosphere, placed markers, and a corrected thin hatch footer carry the visual idea.
questions: Should the footer copy remain in the markup if it is intentionally hidden by the v2.1 correction?
Is the CSS-only light pool intended as a temporary placeholder for generated imagery, as the comment suggests?
Are the hotspot dots meant to be interactive in a later version or only visual markers here?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and comments indicate a warm immersive v2 design test, and the file implements that as a static CSS-composed hero screen with a later footer correction.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to prototype a warm, immersive Studio V design direction using CSS gradients, spatial annotations, and restrained interface elements as a stand-in for eventual generated imagery.
novelty: A late override converts a fuller explanatory footer band into a thin gold hatch while preserving the earlier footer markup, making the revision history visible inside the file.
contribution: Contributes a warm immersive visual substrate with golden-hour gradient lighting, masked blueprint texture, Studio V identity elements, spatial hotspot markers, an informational callout, and a compact hatched grounding band that a fused system could keep as a distinct atmospheric treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS Studio V warm immersive hero mockup with CSS light-pool background, blueprint texture, mission typography, hotspot markers, info card, menu pill, monogram, and corrected thin hatch footer.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for testing a warm atelier-style immersive design direction using CSS-generated atmosphere and placed interface details before relying on generated imagery.
